title: rules.rs: All rule definitions have empty fields arrays (incomplete implementation)
description:
File: rust/lichen-schc/src/rules.rs
Lines: 59-96

Category: CORRECTNESS / API MISUSE

All the rule constants (LINK_LOCAL_COAP_RULE, GLOBAL_COAP_RULE, ICMPV6_ECHO_RULE, RPL_DIO_RULE, RPL_DAO_RULE, LINK_LOCAL_OSCORE_RULE, GLOBAL_OSCORE_RULE, MQTT_SN_RULE) have empty `fields: &[]` arrays:
```rust
pub const LINK_LOCAL_COAP_RULE: Rule = Rule {
    rule_id: RULE_LINK_LOCAL_COAP,
    fields: &[],  // Empty!
};
```

The actual compression/decompression in codec.rs has inline implementations that don't use these rules. This causes:
1. The context.rs rule_matches() function will always match any fields against these rules (empty fields = no constraints)
2. The PacketProfile implementations reference these rules but they have no field descriptors
3. Maintenance hazard: two parallel implementations of the same compression logic

Impact: The SchcContext::select_rule() cannot properly select rules based on field matching since all rules have no field constraints. Anyone using the rule model directly gets incorrect behavior.

Fix: Either populate the field descriptors to match the inline codec.rs logic, or remove the rules model if it's not being used.
